title: Best Road Trip Snacks for Kids in NZ — No-Mess, No-Meltdown Options
meta_desc: Discover the best road trip snacks for kids in NZ, with no-mess and no-meltdown options from $2.50
intro: Long road trips with kids can be daunting, but with the right snacks, you can avoid meltdowns and keep everyone happy. For a 3-hour drive from Auckland to Hamilton, you'll want snacks that are easy to eat, won't make a mess, and can be bought at most supermarkets. We've got you covered with our top picks for road trip snacks in NZ.

sections.heading: Practical Snack Ideas for Long Drives
sections.body: When choosing snacks for a long drive, consider options that are easy to eat, nutritious, and won't leave a mess. Some great options include sandwiches, fruit, and energy balls. You can find these snacks at most supermarkets like Countdown or New World, with prices starting from $2.50 for a packet of sandwiches.

sections.heading: Cooler Tips for Fresh Snacks
sections.body: To keep your snacks fresh during a long drive, consider investing in a cooler. You can buy a cooler at most outdoor stores like Kathmandu or Macpac, with prices starting from $50. When packing your cooler, make sure to include ice packs and plenty of water to keep everyone hydrated.

sections.heading: Avoiding Meltdowns on Long Drives
sections.body: To avoid meltdowns on long drives, make sure to take regular breaks and give your kids a chance to stretch their legs. You can stop at rest stops or parks, like the Hamilton Lake Domain, which is about a 3-hour drive from Auckland. During these breaks, offer your kids a snack and some water to keep them refreshed.
